title: Crime TV
subtitle: Crime drama from HBO prestige to British cozy mystery to international procedural.

items.title: True Detective S1
items.pitch: Louisiana detectives across two timelines investigate ritualistic murders. McConaughey + Harrelson. Cary Fukunaga directed all 8 episodes.

critics.quote: S1 considered one of the great seasons of TV. S2-S4 anthology, varying quality. S4 (Night Country) won the Emmy in 2024.

items.title: The Wire
items.seasons: S1 in progress. S2 polarizing (docks), S3-4 transcendent (politics, schools), S5 weakest (media). Push through S2.
items.pitch: Baltimore drug trade, police, docks, politics, schools, media. Each season tackles a different institution.

critics.quote: Routinely ranked the greatest TV series ever made by critics polls.

items.title: The Sopranos
items.seasons: All 6 (ended 2007). 2021 prequel film The Many Saints of Newark.
items.pitch: New Jersey mob boss in therapy. The show that started prestige TV.

critics.quote: 21 Emmys. The 'Pine Barrens' episode and the cut-to-black finale are TV-history-grade.

items.title: Dexter
items.seasons: S1-S4 are peak (S4 widely considered the best season). S5-8 declined. New Blood (2021) was the closing chapter; Original Sin (2024) is a prequel.
items.pitch: Miami forensic blood-spatter analyst is a vigilante serial killer. Michael C. Hall.

critics.quote: S4 with Lithgow as Trinity widely considered one of TV's great single seasons.

items.title: Weeds
items.pitch: Suburban widow becomes a marijuana dealer. Mary-Louise Parker. The structural ancestor of Breaking Bad and Ozark.

critics.quote: Showtime's first prestige hit. Parker won the Globe in 2006.

items.title: Rosemary & Thyme
items.pitch: Two middle-aged British gardeners solve murders. Cozy mystery in the Midsomer Murders register.

critics.quote: British staple. Beloved by cozy-mystery devotees.

items.title: Cadfael
items.pitch: Derek Jacobi as a 12th-century Welsh Benedictine monk who solves crimes. Adapted from Ellis Peters novels.

critics.quote: Considered the standard for medieval-mystery adaptation. Jacobi's performance carries it.

sections.name: II. High Priority — Recommended
sections.desc: Strong direct fits. Ended series leaning into your established taste.

items.title: Better Call Saul
items.whyPriority: Cross-listed primary placement in Cons & Courtroom TV. Listed here because it's also the strongest crime TV recommendation. Slow-burn character study unmatched in modern TV.
items.pitch: Breaking Bad prequel/sequel. Jimmy McGill's transformation into Saul Goodman, plus what came after.

critics.quote: 53 Emmy nominations across run with zero wins — among the most decorated never-winners. Final season widely considered Breaking Bad's equal.

items.title: Mare of Easttown
items.whyPriority: HBO. Kate Winslet as a small-town Pennsylvania detective. Tight, rooted, devastating. Direct fit for True Detective S1 sensibility.
items.pitch: A Philly-suburb detective investigates a murder in her hometown while her own life unravels.

critics.quote: Won 4 Emmys including Lead Actress (Winslet) and Supporting Actress (Sevigny).

items.title: The Night Of
items.whyPriority: Cross-listed primary placement in Cons & Courtroom TV. Also fits here as crime drama.
items.pitch: Pakistani-American college student is accused of a murder he doesn't remember committing. John Turturro is unforgettable.

critics.quote: Five Emmys including Lead Actor (Riz Ahmed).

items.title: Top of the Lake
items.whyPriority: Jane Campion (The Power of the Dog) directed. Elisabeth Moss as a NZ detective investigating the disappearance of a pregnant 12-year-old. Slow, elemental, devastating.
items.pitch: A pregnant 12-year-old goes missing in remote New Zealand. The detective sent to investigate has her own buried trauma there.

critics.quote: Critical darling. S1 routinely listed among the best limited series of the 2010s.

items.title: Line of Duty
items.seasons: All 6 (last 2021); UK pacing — each season is one investigation
items.whyPriority: BBC. Anti-corruption police unit investigates corrupt cops. Procedurally tight, increasingly conspiracy-laden across seasons.
items.pitch: AC-12 investigates police corruption. Every season builds to one extended interrogation scene that's consistently great TV.

critics.quote: Most-watched UK drama in years during S6. Considered one of the great procedural runs.

items.title: Broadchurch
items.whyPriority: British coastal town, child murder investigation. Tennant + Colman. The grief of the town is the show.
items.pitch: An eleven-year-old is found dead on a beach. A mismatched detective pair investigate. The town is the suspect pool.

critics.quote: Olivia Colman's Emmy/BAFTA-winning performance launched her US career.

sections.name: III. Medium Priority
sections.desc: Strong probable fits. Solid second-tier and adjacent territory.

items.title: Justified
items.pitch: Cross-listed Drama TV. Elmore Leonard adaptation. Timothy Olyphant as a US Marshal in eastern Kentucky.

items.title: Fargo (TV)
items.whyPriority: Each season Coens-flavored crime story standalone. Tonally exact match for your Coen drama loves.
items.pitch: Anthology. Each season a different time period and cast. Snowy Midwest. Sudden violence. Dry humor.

critics.quote: Won Outstanding Limited Series in 2014.

items.title: The Shield
items.whyPriority: FX cop drama. Michael Chiklis as a corrupt LA strike team leader. Considered the show that established FX as a prestige network.
items.pitch: Vic Mackey runs a corrupt anti-gang strike team in LA. The pilot's final scene set the tone for everything FX has done since.

critics.quote: Series finale routinely cited among the greatest in TV history.

items.title: Bosch
items.runtime: 7 seasons · 68 episodes + Bosch: Legacy spin-off ongoing
items.whyPriority: Michael Connelly novels. Titus Welliver as Hieronymus Bosch, LAPD homicide. Procedural and patient.
items.pitch: Bosch works homicide in Hollywood Division. Cases unfold slowly across seasons.

critics.quote: Amazon's first major drama hit. Connelly involved as showrunner kept the books' DNA.

items.title: Ozark
items.pitch: Cross-listed Drama TV. Cartel money laundering in the Missouri Ozarks.

items.title: The Americans
items.pitch: Cross-listed Spy TV / Drama TV. KGB sleeper agents in 1980s suburban DC.

items.title: Bordertown / Sorjonen
items.whyPriority: Finnish. Brilliant detective with Asperger-coded behavior moves to a Russian-border town. Slow Nordic mood, twisty cases. Netflix carries it internationally.
items.pitch: Detective Kari Sorjonen relocates his family to Lappeenranta on the Russian border. He has a near-photographic memory and a daughter with health issues.

critics.quote: Routinely ranked among the best Nordic noir. Finland's most-exported drama.

items.title: The Killing (Forbrydelsen)
items.seasons: All 3 (ended 2012). Danish original; the AMC remake is also strong but Danish version is canonical.
items.pitch: Copenhagen detective Sarah Lund investigates one murder per season. Slow, methodical, atmospheric. Definitive Nordic noir.

critics.quote: BAFTA Best International. Established Nordic noir as a brand.

items.title: The Bridge (Bron/Broen)
items.pitch: A body is found exactly on the border between Sweden and Denmark on the Øresund Bridge. Two detectives — one Swedish, one Danish — must investigate together.

critics.quote: Spawned UK/French and US/Mexico remakes. Original remains the strongest.

items.title: Trapped (Ófærð)
items.pitch: Icelandic small-town police chief investigates crimes amid impossible weather. Visually stunning, sense-of-place dense.

critics.quote: Iceland's most successful international TV export.

items.title: Babylon Berlin
items.dir: Tom Tykwer, Achim von Borries, Henk Handloegten
items.whyPriority: Weimar-era Berlin police drama. Most expensive German TV production ever. Period detail unparalleled. Tom Tykwer (Run Lola Run) directs.
items.pitch: 1929 Berlin. A Cologne police detective investigates corruption in the world's most decadent city as the Nazis rise.

critics.quote: International critical favorite. The Charleston club sequence and the New Year's Eve sequence are among the great TV setpieces of the 2010s.

items.title: Money Heist (La Casa de Papel)
items.pitch: Cross-listed Cons & Courtroom TV. Spanish heist series; the structural construction of the heist is the appeal.

critics.quote: Most-watched non-English Netflix series in history.

sections.name: V. Cozy / British Mystery
sections.desc: Adjacent to your Cadfael / Rosemary & Thyme territory.

items.title: Father Brown (2013)
items.pitch: Mark Williams as G.K. Chesterton's Catholic priest detective in 1950s Cotswolds. Long-running cozy.

critics.quote: BBC daytime workhorse. Modest critical reception, devoted fan base.

items.title: Endeavour
items.whyPriority: Inspector Morse prequel. Shaun Evans as young Endeavour Morse in 1960s-70s Oxford. Higher production values and tighter writing than nearly any cozy.
items.pitch: Young Detective Constable Morse begins his career in Oxford. Each episode a feature-length mystery.

critics.quote: Critical darling across nine seasons. Beloved by critics for being the rare prequel that equaled or exceeded its parent (Inspector Morse).

items.title: Inspector Morse
items.pitch: John Thaw as Chief Inspector Morse in Oxford. Originating series before Endeavour and Lewis.

critics.quote: Considered the gold standard British detective drama.

items.title: Lewis (Inspector Lewis)
items.pitch: Morse's former sergeant Lewis is now a detective inspector. Kevin Whately. Same Oxford setting.

critics.quote: Critical reputation just below Endeavour and Morse, but routinely strong.

items.title: Vera
items.pitch: Blethyn as DCI Vera Stanhope in Northumberland. Long-running, atmospheric, character-anchored.

items.title: Shetland
items.pitch: Detective Inspector Jimmy Perez on the Shetland Islands. Original lead Douglas Henshall left in 2022; the show continued.
